sn12.44:1.2: “Bhikkhus, I will teach the origination and the ending of the world.
sn12.44:1.3: Listen to that, pay close attention, I will speak.”
sn12.44:2.1: “And what, bhikkhus, is the origination of the world?
sn12.44:2.2: Dependent on the eye and *rūpas*, eye-consciousness [*cakkhuviññāṇaṁ*] arises. The meeting of the three is contact.
sn12.44:2.8: from birth as condition, aging and death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, sadness, and distress come to be.
sn12.44:2.9: This, bhikkhus, is the origination of the world.
sn12.44:3.1: Dependent on the ear and sounds …
sn12.44:3.2: dependent on the nose and odors …
sn12.44:3.3: dependent on the tongue and tastes …
sn12.44:3.4: dependent on the body and tangibles …
sn12.44:3.5: dependent on the mind and *dhammas*, mind-consciousness [*manoviññāṇaṁ*] arises. The meeting of the three is contact.
sn12.44:3.7: from birth as condition, aging and death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, sadness, and distress come to be.
sn12.44:3.8: This, bhikkhus, is the origination of the world.
sn12.44:4.1: And what, bhikkhus, is the ending of the world?
sn12.44:4.2: Dependent on the eye and *rūpas*, eye-consciousness [*cakkhuviññāṇaṁ*] arises. The meeting of the three is contact.
sn12.44:4.5: From the remainderless fading away and cessation of that very *taṇhā*, cessation of clinging [*upādānanirodho*];
sn12.44:4.6: from the cessation of clinging [*upādānanirodhā*], cessation of *bhava* [*bhavanirodho*] …
sn12.44:4.7: thus there is the cessation of this whole mass of *dukkha* [*dukkhakkhandhassa*].
sn12.44:4.8: This, bhikkhus, is the ending of the world.
sn12.44:5.1: Dependent on the ear and sounds …
sn12.44:5.10: thus there is the cessation of this whole mass of *dukkha* [*dukkhakkhandhassa*].
sn12.44:5.11: This, bhikkhus, is the ending of the world.”
sn12.44:5.2: dependent on the nose and odors …
sn12.44:5.3: dependent on the tongue and tastes …
sn12.44:5.4: dependent on the body and tangibles …
sn12.44:5.5: dependent on the mind and *dhammas*, mind-consciousness [*manoviññāṇaṁ*] arises. The meeting of the three is contact.
sn12.44:5.8: From the remainderless fading away and cessation of that very *taṇhā*, cessation of clinging [*upādānanirodho*];
sn12.44:5.9: from the cessation of clinging [*upādānanirodhā*], cessation of *bhava* [*bhavanirodho*] …
